notes: with_skill runs achieved 100% pass rate across all 3 evals — skill provides strong, structured workflow
without_skill pass rate is 17-40% depending on eval — ideas can be generated but scripts/file output/Japanese prompt all fail
Eval 2 without_skill scores higher (40%) because demographic targeting and scoring tables are present (different axes but similar structure)
with_skill uses far fewer tokens (913 avg vs 33,539 avg) because structured scripts replace exploratory WebSearch loops
The Japanese year confirmation assertion (Eval 3) is 100% discriminating — always passes with skill, always fails without
Script execution assertions are highly discriminating — zero false positives in without_skill runs
Scoring table assertion in Eval 2 is partially non-discriminating — without_skill also generates tables (different axes). Consider tightening.
